questions.q: In which Indian state is the Modhera Sun Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Modhera Sun Temple stands in the village of Modhera in the Mehsana district of Gujarat.

questions.q: To which deity is the Modhera Sun Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Surya, the Hindu Sun god, after whom it is named the Sun Temple.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple stands on the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: The temple complex was built on the bank of the Pushpavati River at Modhera.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple is famous for its large stepped tank. What is this tank called?
questions.explanation: The temple's grand rectangular stepped tank is known as the Surya Kund or Ramakund.

questions.q: During which centuries was the Modhera Sun Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple was constructed in the early 11th century during the Solanki (Chaulukya) period.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple was built during the rule of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by the Solanki (Chaulukya) dynasty, who were sun worshippers.

questions.q: What primary building material was used to construct the Modhera Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was carved from soft yellowish-pink sandstone, giving it its distinctive golden hue.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple is celebrated as a masterpiece of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The temple is a fine example of the Maru-Gurjara style associated with the Solanki rulers.

questions.q: A notable feature of the temple is its design so that sunlight strikes the sanctum at certain times. At which times does this occur?
questions.explanation: The temple is aligned so the rising sun illuminates the sanctum at the equinoxes.

questions.q: Today the Modhera Sun Temple is maintained and protected as a monument by which body?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) maintains the Modhera Sun Temple as a protected monument.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple is traditionally attributed to which Solanki king?
questions.explanation: The temple is traditionally credited to King Bhima I of the Solanki dynasty in the early 11th century.

questions.q: An inscription on a wall of the temple points to a construction date corresponding to which year?
questions.explanation: An inscription bearing the date Vikram Samvat 1083 corresponds to around 1027 CE.

questions.q: What is the name of the assembly or dance hall that lies between the tank and the main shrine?
questions.explanation: The pillared assembly hall, also called the Sabha Mandapa or Nritya Mandapa, stands before the main shrine.

questions.q: The main shrine building of the Modhera Sun Temple is known by which term?
questions.explanation: The main temple hall housing the sanctum is the Gudha Mandapa, connected to the inner sanctum.

questions.q: Roughly how many small shrines line the steps of the temple's Surya Kund tank?
questions.explanation: The Surya Kund is famously lined with about 108 miniature shrines along its tiered steps.

questions.q: An ornate arched gateway, or torana, stands at the temple. What is such a structure called?
questions.explanation: A torana is a decorative ceremonial archway; Modhera features such carved toranas at its halls.

questions.q: The carvings around the sanctum traditionally depict twelve forms of Surya representing what?
questions.explanation: Twelve images of Surya (the twelve Adityas) symbolize the twelve months of the year.

questions.q: Which medieval invader is generally blamed for damaging the Modhera Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is widely held to have been plundered and damaged during the raids of Mahmud of Ghazni.

questions.q: Worship is no longer performed at the Modhera Sun Temple. What is its current primary status?
questions.explanation: Active worship has ceased; the temple is preserved as a protected archaeological and heritage monument.

questions.q: Modhera village lies closest to which major Gujarat district headquarters town?
questions.explanation: Modhera is located in the Mehsana district, near the town of Mehsana in northern Gujarat.

questions.q: A popular cultural festival of classical dance is held annually at Modhera. What is it generally called?
questions.explanation: The Uttarardha Mahotsav (Modhera Dance Festival) showcases classical dance against the temple backdrop.

questions.q: In 2022, Modhera village gained national attention for becoming India's first of what kind?
questions.explanation: In 2022 Modhera was inaugurated as India's first round-the-clock solar-powered village.

questions.q: The Solanki dynasty that built Modhera ruled from which capital city?
questions.explanation: The Solankis (Chaulukyas) ruled Gujarat from their capital at Anhilwad Patan, modern Patan.

questions.q: The Modhera Sun Temple is often compared in design and purpose to which other famous Indian sun temple?
questions.explanation: It is frequently compared to the later Konark Sun Temple in Odisha, also dedicated to Surya.

questions.q: The Maru-Gurjara architectural style of Modhera is also strongly associated with which religion's temples in western India?
questions.explanation: The Maru-Gurjara (Solanki) style is also seen in famous Jain temples like Dilwara and Ranakpur.

questions.q: The Surya Kund at Modhera is also popularly known by which alternative name?
questions.explanation: The stepped temple tank is also commonly called the Ramakund.

questions.q: The Solanki kings who built Modhera belonged to a clan often counted among which group of Rajput clans?
questions.explanation: The Chaulukyas (Solankis) are traditionally counted among the Agnivanshi Rajput clans.

questions.q: Which feature of the Gudha Mandapa's exterior most clearly reflects the temple's solar dedication?
options: A series of carved Surya images facing the cardinal directions
A reflecting marble pool inside the sanctum
questions.explanation: Carved Surya figures placed to catch the sun reinforce the temple's dedication to the Sun god.

questions.q: The intricate ceiling and pillars of the Modhera Sabha Mandapa are most renowned for what?
options: Densely carved figurative and floral relief work
questions.explanation: The hall is famed for its profuse, finely carved stone relief of deities, dancers, and floral motifs.

questions.q: Although a landmark of Indian temple architecture, what is the Modhera Sun Temple's status with respect to UNESCO World Heritage listing?
options: A full World Heritage Site since 1985
On the UNESCO Tentative List, not yet inscribed
questions.explanation: The Modhera Sun Temple is on India's UNESCO World Heritage Tentative List but is not yet a full inscribed site.
